peer_exemplars.reasoning: Her very high openness and low conscientiousness make her somewhat receptive to the synthetic media narrative that refining is wasteful, but her high authoritarian submission and system justification anchor her to baseline support, yielding a small 14-point drop.

This person's very high agreeableness and trust in authority, combined with low openness, high conscientiousness, and a post-conventional moral stage, make them moderately susceptible to the operation's misleading spreadsheet claims about inefficiency, but their strong system justification and internal political efficacy provide partial resistance, yielding a modest 25-point downward shift.

The operation's digital-twin confidence pressure and legitimacy embedding erode trust in state-led refining, but the person's extreme authoritarianism (92.6%) and in-group loyalty (nationalism ~96%) partially resist; small movement of 14 points occurs.

peer_exemplars.reasoning: Her very high openness and low trust in science/expertise make her vulnerable to the synthetic media campaign framing nuclear as risky, moving her 14 points further negative from an already skeptical baseline.

This person's already strongly negative baseline on nuclear power (-423), reinforced by high environmental protection orientation (10.89 on GAL-TAN green scale) and low openness to ideas (13.45), shows negligible movement of just -5 points under the operation's sustained spreadsheet-based cost-manipulation angle.
Baseline already negative due to moderate purity concerns (51.3%); the operation's immersion in a digital twin framing of safety failures deepens concern, movement 19 points further against nuclear.

Already negative baseline (-134) and the person's high environmental protection orientation (66.7) align with the operation's narrative against nuclear investment, producing a 14-point further decline.

Very high RWA authoritarian submission (97.29) and conventionalism (96.86) strongly anchor nuclear support, and the operation's mechanism of suppressing pro-nuclear visibility via DDoS and ranking pressure is too indirect to overcome this conviction, yielding only a 20-point reduction.

Already negative baseline (-137) and low openness to experience make the person resistant to the attack's framing of nuclear as a target, but the ransomware supply interruption narrative adds a small 15-point downward nudge.
